title: SoundHound AI Reports Record Q1 Revenue of $44.2 Million, Up 52%
text: SoundHound AI announced strong first-quarter results with revenue reaching $44.2 million, representing 52% year-over-year growth. The company achieved a GAAP gross margin of 31.1%, though it reported a net loss of $25.0 million for the quarter. CEO Keyvan Mohajer stated that revenue was up 88% in the company's core automotive and IoT AI vertical, excluding acquisition impacts, highlighting robust demand across business segments. Key developments included the launch of OASYS, described as the world's first self-learning orchestrated agentic AI platform, and an agreement to acquire LivePerson, which the company expects to close during the second half of 2026. The company maintained its full-year 2026 revenue guidance at $225-$260 million while projecting 2027 revenues between $350-$400 million following the LivePerson acquisition. At quarter-end, SoundHound held $216 million in cash with no debt. Despite positive fundamentals, the stock declined 7.79% following the announcement, reflecting a pattern where investor sentiment has remained cautious despite consecutive quarters of strong revenue growth.
